# Daily Report — 2026-04-11

## Task Completed
Implemented `create_catalog_variable_choice` tool.

Select-type catalog item variables (dropdowns, radio buttons, checkboxes) get
their options from the ServiceNow `question_choice` table.  The new tool POST
to `/api/now/table/question_choice`, linking a choice record to a variable via
the `question` field (sys_id of the `item_option_new` variable record).

Fields supported:
- `variable_id` (required) — sys_id of the parent variable
- `text` (required) — display label shown to users
- `value` (required) — internal stored value
- `order` (optional) — display order
- `price` / `price_type` (optional) — price modifier with type `flat_fee` or `one_time`
- `inactive` (optional, default false) — disable the choice without deleting it

The tool and its params/response models were added to:
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/catalog_variables.py`
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/__init__.py`
- `src/servicenow_mcp/utils/tool_utils.py`
- `config/tool_packages.yaml` (enabled in `catalog_builder` and `full` packages)
- `tests/test_catalog_variables.py` (6 new tests)

## Test Results
- Total tests: 400 passed, 4 deselected
- Coverage: 87% overall; `catalog_variables.py` at 94%
- All passing: yes

## Security
Pre-existing environment vulnerabilities in cryptography, mcp, pip, pyjwt,
setuptools, and wheel — none introduced by this change.

## Files Changed
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/catalog_variables.py`
